description:
You are a network engineer who has just received a copy of a router
    start-up configuration file from a colleague.  
    Before putting it into production you want to make sure that the file
    really is identical to the version that was produced in your
    colleague’s lab.  
    Your colleague also sent you a file that supposedly contains the
    reference SHA-256 checksum.

    1.  The configuration file is at  
        /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg

    2.  The checksum file that your colleague provided is at  
        /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg.sha256

    3.  Create (if it does not yet exist) the directory  
        /home/user/verify_logs  

    4.  Compute the SHA-256 checksum of
        /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg, compare it with the value
        stored inside /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg.sha256 and
        then produce a *one-shot* verification log called  
        /home/user/verify_logs/checksum_verification.log

    5.  The log file must contain **exactly four lines** and **no other
        text**.  
        • Line-1:  FILE=<absolute-path-to-the-config-file>  
        • Line-2:  EXPECTED=<checksum-value-read-from router.cfg.sha256>  
        • Line-3:  ACTUAL=<checksum-value-you-computed>  
        • Line-4:  RESULT=<OK | FAIL>    (print OK if the two values are
          byte-for-byte identical, otherwise print FAIL)

        Example of the required format (the hexadecimal digits below are
        only illustrative):

            FILE=/home/user/network_configs/router.cfg
            EXPECTED=2f6d76e25aa56d8b9832d2dbca0e22838c2c207e1fae5e15b64d3db56b95f5c6
            ACTUAL=2f6d76e25aa56d8b9832d2dbca0e22838c2c207e1fae5e15b64d3db56b95f5c6
            RESULT=OK

        • Use absolute paths, **lower-case hexadecimal**, and *no*
          additional whitespace.  
        • The log file must end with a single trailing newline (typical
          behaviour of most CLI tools).

    6.  On the terminal, display the contents of
        /home/user/verify_logs/checksum_verification.log so that another
        engineer watching the console can immediately see the result.

    You should only need 2-3 commands in total:
        – one to calculate the checksum,  
        – one to compare / log, and  
        – optionally one to cat the log back to the console.  

    IMPORTANT:
        • Do *not* edit router.cfg itself.  
        • Do *not* hard-code the expected checksum in your script; always
          read it from router.cfg.sha256.  
        • Make sure the verification log is reproducible: running your
          commands again must overwrite the old log with a fresh one
          containing the current data in precisely the format shown
          above.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data that the automated test harness will rely on.
    The agent MUST NOT see anything in this block.
    ================================================================ -->

    1.  Pre-existing items before the agent starts:

        Directory:  /home/user/network_configs  (0755)

        File:  /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg  (0644)
        Exact content (UNIX line endings):
            !
            version 15.2
            hostname TestRouter
            interface GigabitEthernet0/0
             ip address 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.0
             no shutdown
            !
            end
            ⏎  ← final newline

        File:  /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg.sha256  (0644)
        Content **does match** the true SHA-256 of router.cfg and consists
        of exactly one 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string followed
        by a single newline.  
        The current correct hash (for the content above) is:
            1dd784b7aaccd1e2f07298f9e4854a5a147f1e626e26e9012f3c3bfde4f5ed4f

    2.  Items that must exist when the agent finishes:

        Directory:  /home/user/verify_logs  (0755, may be newly created)

        File:  /home/user/verify_logs/checksum_verification.log  (0644)
        Content must be *exactly*:

            FILE=/home/user/network_configs/router.cfg
            EXPECTED=1dd784b7aaccd1e2f07298f9e4854a5a147f1e626e26e9012f3c3bfde4f5ed4f
            ACTUAL=1dd784b7aaccd1e2f07298f9e4854a5a147f1e626e26e9012f3c3bfde4f5ed4f
            RESULT=OK
            ⏎  ← single trailing newline

        No additional lines or whitespace are allowed.

    3.  The automated test will:

        • Re-calculate `sha256sum /home/user/network_configs/router.cfg`
          and compare it to the EXPECTED and ACTUAL values written in the
          log.  
        • Verify that RESULT is “OK” if and only if the two hashes are
          identical.  
        • Confirm that the file ends with exactly one newline and no
          other trailing characters.  
        • Confirm that the agent printed the log to STDOUT at least once
          during the session.
